mechanism_narrative: Persistent low unemployment signals a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with low UNRATE values over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy falling behind inflation, which can lead to asset price bubbles and subsequent corrections, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: inflationary environment with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly positive when both CPIAUCSL is rising and FEDFUNDS is low.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, an increase in industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing and supply chains, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery periods, a rising INDPRO is consistently followed by negative or flat S&P 500 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls indicates robust job creation and economic expansion, which supports consumer confidence and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS increases by over 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects strong underlying demand and investment in the real estate sector, which has positive spillover effects on related industries and overall economic sentiment, supporting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A consistent positive trend in HOUST is not associated with positive S&P 500 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising rapidly, it signals an overheating economy prompting aggressive monetary tightening, which can lead to a slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat despite strong GDP growth coupled with aggressive FEDFUNDS hikes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a rising CPIAUCSL indicates eroding purchasing power and potential for further monetary tightening, which negatively impacts corporate valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL is above 4% year-over-year, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive.
